name: Frugal Gourmet's Sesame Candy
recipeInstructions:
1. Do not make candy on a hot or humid day.
2. In a deep, medium-size saucepan, slowly heat honey over medium-low heat using a heat defuser until it reaches the hard-crack stage (about 305°F to 310°F on a candy thermometer. Stir down often to prevent boiling over, about 45 minutes.
3. Stir in sesame seeds. Set aside for a few minutes to cool slightly.
4. Lightly grease a 9 x 13 inch baking sheet with peanut oil. Do not use waxed paper.
5. Pour honey mixture onto the baking sheet and set it on a cooling rack.
6. When cooled, but not hard, score into diamond shapes.
7. When completely cooled, remove candy and separate into pieces.
This is listed as a Saudi Arabian recipe from Frugal Gourmet, "On Our Immigrant Ancestors" by Jeff Smith p. 479.
